{{wikidata link|Q11669562}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takaoka Sekino Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Takaoka Sekino shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '富山県氷見市神代', 'language': 'ja'} == significant event (P793) == * {{ill|Q6663970|lt=Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} {{commons category|Category:Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} [[JA:高岡関野神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669562}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takaoka Sekino Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Takaoka Sekino shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11669562}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takaoka Sekino Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Takaoka Sekino shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11669562}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takaoka Sekino Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Takaoka Sekino shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'富山県高岡市末広町9-56', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '富山県氷見市神代', 'language': 'ja'} == significant event (P793) == * {{ill|Q6663970|ltq=Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} {{commons category|Category:Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} [[JA:高岡関野神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669562}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takaoka Sekino Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Takaoka Sekino shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988|1=Takaoka, Toyama}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742|1=Maeda Toshinaga}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho|1=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988|1=Takaoka, Toyama}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami|1=Inari Ōkami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742|1=Maeda Toshinaga}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886|1=Himi, Toyama}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738|1=1604}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628|1=Mitoda}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu|1=Kumano Gongen}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765|1=1610}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764|1=Takaoka Castle}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448|1=Kaga Domain}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903|1=1806}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618|1=June 9}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429|1=1987}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613|1=2026}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho|1=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988|1=Takaoka, Toyama}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami|1=Inari Ōkami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742|1=Maeda Toshinaga}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886|1=Himi, Toyama}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738|1=1604}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628|1=Mitoda}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu|1=Kumano Gongen}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765|1=1610}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764|1=Takaoka Castle}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448|1=Kaga Domain}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903|1=1806}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618|1=June 9}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429|1=1987}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613|1=2026}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Festival}} {{commons category|Category:Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} [[JA:高岡関野神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669562}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Takaoka Sekino Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Takaoka Sekino shrine.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988|1=Takaoka, Toyama}}Suehiro-cho 9-56 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|36.7|N|137|0|41.1|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742|1=Maeda Toshinaga}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}} ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takaoka
Sekino Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takaoka Sekino Shrine''' (たかおかせきのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Suehiro-cho (Takaoka)|Q135477385|WD=Q135477385|lt=Suehiro-cho|1=Suehiro-cho}}, {{ill|lt=Takaoka|Q502988|WD=Q502988|1=Takaoka, Toyama}}, {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. The old orthography is '''Takaoka Sekino Jinja''' (關野), and another name is '''Taka-no-miya'''. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === There are three main sanctuary buildings, each enshrining the following deities. * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amateru Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Mikenuma-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Mikeiri-no-Mikoto}}) * Kumano Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, Kotosakanoo-no-Mikoto, Hayamizu-no-Mikoto), with aiden deities (Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Kagutsuchi) * Inari Shrine ({{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura-tama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} ({{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Omikami|1=Inari Ōkami}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Maeda Toshinaga|Q1186742|WD=Q1186742|1=Maeda Toshinaga}}) === History === Until the Edo period, Takaoka Sekino Shrine consisted of three shrines - Kakumi Shrine (Shinmei Shrine), Sekino Shrine (Kumano Shrine), and Takaoka Shrine (Inari Shrine) - collectively called the Sekino Three Shrines. '''Kakumi Shrine''' (かくみじんじゃ) is said to have been a shikinaisha small shrine in Kamiseki Village, Sekino-go (present Kamiseki, Takaoka). However, another candidate for Kakumi Shrine exists in Kojiro, {{ill|lt=Himi|Q646886|WD=Q646886|1=Himi, Toyama}}, and there is a tradition that when this shrine attempted to raise a plaque reading "Kakumi," parishioners from Himi's Kakumi Shrine came and prevented its use. '''Sekino Shrine''' (せきのじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|WD=Q6738|1=1604}} (Keicho 9), when a divided spirit was transferred from Kumano-san Mitsuzo-ji in {{ill|lt=Mitoda Village|Q11548628|WD=Q11548628|1=Mitoda}} (present {{ill|lt=Imizu|Q851411|WD=Q851411|1=Imizu}} Mitoda 3934), {{ill|lt=Imizu District|Q6004195|WD=Q6004195|1=Imizu District, Toyama}}, and enshrined as {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano-gu|1=Kumano Gongen}}[https://yaokami.jp/1166527/]、[http://www.mikumano.net/ztoyama/daimon1.html National Kumano Shrine Pilgrimage Record]、[http://www.kimura-product.co.jp/hudou1/hokurikuhudo36/hokurikuhudo3605.htm (Hokuriku 36 Fudo sacred sites)]. '''Takaoka Shrine''' (たかおかじんじゃ) began in {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765|1=1610}} (Keicho 15), when Maeda Toshinaga invited Inari Omikami as castle guardian during construction of {{ill|lt=Takaoka Castle|Q8011764|WD=Q8011764|1=Takaoka Castle}}. It received devotion from the ruling {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Maeda family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Kaga Domain|Q903448|WD=Q903448|1=Kaga Domain}}, and later Toshinaga's spirit was also enshrined. In {{ill|lt=1806|Q6903|WD=Q6903|1=1806}} (Bunka 3), Kakumi Shrine, Sekino Shrine, and Takaoka Shrine were moved to the current site and enshrined in one shared precinct. In Meiji, Kakumi Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine and ranked as a gosha. Takaoka Shrine became a prefectural shrine. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taisho 8), Takaoka Shrine was merged into Sekino Shrine, creating the prefectural Takaoka Sekino Shrine. === Festivals === The annual festival on {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}, called the "{{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}}," features seven festival floats known as "Mikurumayama" that parade through central Takaoka. On {{ill|lt=June 9, 1960|Q2618|WD=Q2618|1=June 9}}, the "Takaoka Mikurumayama" was designated an {{ill|lt=Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property|Q11644858|WD=Q11644858|1=National Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property}}. On {{ill|lt=February 3, 1979|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}, "Mikurumayama procession rites of the Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival" were designated an {{ill|lt=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q6573893|WD=Q6573893|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}. === Parishioner areas === {{div col|2}} ; Jozuka district : Miyawaki-cho dori : Miyawaki-cho 1-chome : Suehiro-cho 2-ku : Suehiro-cho 1-ku : Kirigi-cho : Shin-yoko-cho : Chuo-cho : Otaya-cho : Daifukuin-dori : Daibutsu-cho ; Bakuromachi district : Shirogane-cho : Minami-cho 3-ku : Minami-cho 2-ku : Minami-cho 1-ku : Kamogawa-cho : Shimizu-cho 2-ku : Shimizu-cho Kami-ku : Shimizu-cho 1-chome : Shimizu Honmachi : Bakuromachi 1-ku : Hamada-cho : Minami-cho 4-ku : Kamoshima-cho 1-ku : Saiwai-cho : Bakuromachi : Torimachi : Kamoshima-cho : Niban-cho : Toshiya-cho ; Hiraimai district : Takakatabara-cho : Katahara Nakajima-cho : Katahara-cho : Katahara Yoko-cho : Horiage-cho (shrine location) : Moriyama-cho : Kibune-cho : Komadashi-cho : Moto-cho : Hiraimai-cho : Chigiya-cho : Sakashita-cho : Genpei-cho : Sanban-cho : Ichiban-cho : Omadashi-cho ; Seibi district : Otsubo Jiko-cho : Seibi-cho 1-chome : Horo-cho : Abura-cho : Shiokura-cho : Fukuro-cho : Kamiokeya-cho : Tateyoko-cho : Kajiwara-cho ; Shimoseki district : Yoshino-choFrom Takaoka Sekino Shrine newsletter "Sekino no Mori" {{div col end}} === Former Seki Residence === A two-story wooden building (priest residence) east of the shrine, thought to have been built in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. It is owned by the Seki family, hereditary chief priests of Sekino Shrine. It had been vacant since {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|WD=Q2429|1=1987}}, but in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}} the shrine acquired it and began utilizing it in cooperation with local construction company "KDC" and startup support company "Kaiju Ink." From autumn {{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}} onward, it is being opened in stages as a mixed facility including lodging, restaurant, and souvenir shop. On November 1 of that year, a Japanese restaurant "Sekino" by kappo "Jubun" opened; souvenir shop and inn "tumuru" by ritual-goods maker "Hashimoto Kiyoshi" are scheduled for spring {{ill|lt=2026|Q49613|WD=Q49613|1=2026}} (shop) and winter of the same year (inn)From ''Toyama Shimbun'', Sep 20, 2025, p.25.. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{commonscat|Takaoka-Sekino-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival|Q6663970|WD=Q6663970|1=Takaoka Mikurumayama Festival}} === External links === * [https://www.takaokasekinojinjya.com Official site of Takaoka Sekino Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかおかせきのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takaoka Sekino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]
